data.title: Tag management CLI with PIL-guarded brainstorm:complete add
data.description:
## Context

The ticket CLI can write tags at create time (`ticket create --tags`) and edit time (`ticket edit --tags=`, full-list replacement), but has no first-class way to add or remove an individual tag. DSO skills work around this with duplicated read-merge-write logic in at least four places: brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a, design-approve.sh, ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh, and the UI designer dispatch protocol. The one tag that carries a workflow precondition — brainstorm:complete, used by /dso:sprint to filter eligible epics — can be applied to any ticket regardless of whether scrutiny actually ran, producing false positives for sprint eligibility.

## Success Criteria

1. `ticket tag <id> <tag>` adds a single tag to a ticket tag set and preserves all existing tags; command succeeds idempotently when the tag is already present.
2. `ticket untag <id> <tag>` removes a single tag and preserves all other tags; command succeeds idempotently when the tag is already absent. Removing brainstorm:complete returns an epic to a brainstorm-able state — intentionally supported for epics where uncertainty surfaces post-scrutiny.
3. `ticket tag <id> brainstorm:complete` is rejected with a clear, actionable error when no "### Planning Intelligence Log" heading exists in the ticket description or any event (CREATE, EDIT, COMMENT). All other tags add without a PIL check.
4. Brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a and design-approve.sh call the new commands directly instead of open-coding tag-merge logic. Duplicated ~15-line read-merge-write blocks are deleted; new call sites are ≤3 lines each.
5. Tag policy (what is guarded, what is not, why convention is load-bearing) is documented in plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md and cross-referenced from docs/contracts/scrutiny-pending-tag.md and docs/contracts/interaction-deferred-tag.md.
6. A practitioner can complete a round-trip workflow — apply brainstorm:complete, later untag it to re-enter brainstorming, and re-apply it with PIL still present — without any lost tags, errors, or read-merge-write collisions against other fields being edited in the same window.

## Dependencies

None. Related but not blocking: 736d-b957 (stage-boundary preconditions manifest) — future work there may generalize PIL-heading detection as a reusable precondition; the simple check in this epic is compatible with later extension.

## Approach

Option C: Extract tag manipulation into ticket-lib.sh helpers (_tag_add_checked, _tag_remove, reuse _ticket_has_pil from ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh), then ship two thin wrapper scripts ticket-tag.sh and ticket-untag.sh that source the lib. Refactor the 4 existing call sites to use the new commands; document tag policy in ticket-system-v3-architecture.md. No new event types — read-merge-write concurrency race is explicitly accepted as a known limitation (same tolerance as existing ticket edit --tags=).

## Scenario Analysis

Covered by SCs:
- Happy path add/remove (SC1, SC2)
- PIL-guard rejection (SC3)
- Round-trip re-brainstorming (SC6)
- Refactor parity (SC4)

Known limitations documented in SC5 policy:
- Concurrent tag writes by two agents can lose one update (accepted — same tolerance as existing ticket edit --tags=)
- No tag vocabulary validation — arbitrary strings accepted by design (err toward flexibility)
- Source enforcement (only skill X writes tag Y) is not feasible — known ticket-system limitation

Implementation details flagged for /dso:implementation-plan:
- Empty-tag-set behavior when untag removes the last tag (EDIT event with tags: [] vs. field omission)
- PIL detection must scan description across all events (CREATE, EDIT, COMMENT) via reuse of _ticket_has_pil

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none — bash-script addition mirroring existing ticket-edit.sh pattern; no new dependencies, external integration, novel pattern, security surface, or migration concerns
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 4 SC-covered + 3 documented-as-limitation + 2 implementation-detail flags (9 total surfaced)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: not triggered
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — CLI tooling, low invocation frequency
